name: protein_symmetric_homotrimer
description: Redesign of a 32 aa GCN4-zipper-like parallel three-stranded coiled-coil protomer scaffold (heptad-Ile-rich variant in the spirit of the Harbury et al. 1993 Science 262:1401-1407 trimer mutants, PDB 1GCM) under MCMC with ESMFold-based symmetry constraints. The seed sequence has Ile dominating heptad a and d positions to bias the 3-helix oligomerization state; the wild-type GCN4 leucine zipper (with Val@a / Leu@d) is dimeric, while Ile-Ile or Leu-Ile combinations switch the bundle to trimer/tetramer per Harbury 1993. Replace the seed with the literal PDB 1GCM SEQRES if your campaign needs the exact published variant. Constraints: `protein-symmetry-ring(all_to_all_protomer_symmetry=True)` on repeated protomer targets measures all pairwise protomer-protomer distance dispersion when ESMFold-predicting 3 chains, rewarding sequences whose three-fold symmetric assembly has an even ring; `protein-globularity` on the same repeated targets rewards a compact globular trimer; `structure-plddt` and `structure-ptm` reward confidence in the predicted trimer fold. We deliberately omit `balanced-aa` here - heptad design intrinsically biases the sequence toward I, L, K, E at the a/d/e/g positions, which is exactly the pattern `balanced-aa` would penalize as low diversity. Random-protein generator with a 1-mutation step is appropriate because the heptad code is positional - large jumps disrupt the a-d hydrophobic seam.

segments.label: 32 aa GCN4-zipper-like parallel coiled-coil protomer scaffold
